title: Command, Resolution, and the Resolver
purpose: Define the seam through which every world-touching workflow call is decided. The Command and Resolution types are the vocabulary the engine and the durability layer share; the Resolver is the single chokepoint that, against the HistoryCursor, returns a recorded Resolution, reports resume-live, or (in AD-005) raises a violation. Funnelling all calls through one place means recorded-vs-live is decided in exactly one location.
task: Define the Command and Resolution types (command.rs) and the Resolver (resolver.rs). A Command models each world-touching call (run activity, await signal, start timer, spawn child, complete workflow), each carrying its CorrelationKey and any input Payload. A Resolution models each recorded outcome. The Resolver holds a HistoryCursor and, given a Command, maps the cursor's Matched into a Resolution, the cursor's Exhausted into ResumeLive, and (wiring left to AD-005) the cursor's Mismatch into a violation. This brief delivers the happy-path resolution (Matched -> Resolution, Exhausted -> ResumeLive) and the single entry point; AD-005 fills in the Mismatch -> NonDeterminismError path and the dedicated tests.

requirements.title: Command type
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a Command enum modelling the world-touching calls a workflow can issue: RunActivity, AwaitSignal, StartTimer, SpawnChild, and CompleteWorkflow, each carrying its CorrelationKey and (where applicable) its input Payload and activity/workflow type.
acceptance: command.rs defines Command with variants RunActivity { key, activity_type, input }, AwaitSignal { key }, StartTimer { key, fire_at }, SpawnChild { key, workflow_type, input }, CompleteWorkflow { result }.
Each variant's key is a CorrelationKey (from AD-003) consistent with the recorded event it will be matched against.
A unit test constructs one Command of each variant and asserts its CorrelationKey round-trips.

requirements.title: Resolution type
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a Resolution enum modelling the recorded outcomes a Command can resolve to: ActivityCompleted(result Payload), ActivityFailedTerminal(ActivityError), TimerFired, SignalDelivered(payload Payload), ChildCompleted(result Payload), and ChildFailed(WorkflowError).
acceptance: command.rs (or a sibling) defines Resolution with the six variants above plus a ResumeLive sentinel (or ResumeLive is a distinct return value alongside Resolution — pick one and document it).
Each recorded-outcome Resolution carries the aion-core domain value drawn from the recorded event (Payload / ActivityError / WorkflowError).
A unit test constructs each Resolution variant and asserts equality round-trips.

requirements.title: Resolver: Matched -> Resolution, Exhausted -> ResumeLive
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a Resolver holding a HistoryCursor. WHEN given a Command, THE Resolver SHALL consult the cursor for the matching family and key: on Matched it SHALL return the corresponding recorded Resolution; on Exhausted it SHALL return ResumeLive.
acceptance: resolver.rs defines Resolver owning a HistoryCursor (and a reference to the DeterminismContext is wired in AD-006; this brief may take it as a parameter or leave a documented hook).
resolve(command) maps a cursor Matched(recorded events) to the Resolution carrying the recorded domain value (e.g. ActivityCompleted's result Payload).
resolve(command) maps a cursor Exhausted to ResumeLive.

A unit test resolves a RunActivity against a history containing its ActivityCompleted and asserts Resolution::ActivityCompleted with the recorded Payload; a second RunActivity past the recorded end asserts ResumeLive.
The Mismatch arm is present but delegates to a single function whose body is completed in AD-005 (documented as such); it must compile and must not silently return a Resolution.

requirements.title: Single chokepoint for all world-touching calls
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL route every world-touching call through the single Resolver::resolve entry point so that recorded-vs-live is decided in exactly one location. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT provide an alternative path that resolves a Command outside the Resolver.
acceptance: There is exactly one public method (resolve) that maps a Command to a resolution outcome; no per-family resolve_activity / resolve_timer public methods exist that bypass it.
The activity, timer, signal, and child families are all handled inside resolve via the CorrelationKey, not via separate public entry points.
A unit test exercises resolve for one Command of each family against a matching history and asserts the correct Resolution variant for each.

boundaries: SHALL NOT complete the Mismatch -> NonDeterminismError logic or its dedicated tests — that is AD-005 (this brief leaves a single, compiling, non-silent delegation point).
SHALL NOT invoke any LiveExecutor — ResumeLive is a returned signal, not a live call; the handoff is AD-007.
SHALL NOT read the wall clock or generate randomness — determinism inputs are AD-006.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes (command + resolver happy-path tests).
Exactly one public resolve entry point exists (CO19/C19) — grep confirms no bypassing per-family public resolvers.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
